item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: This reflects a deeper cultural emphasis on collective harmony over individual dissent, which can sometimes conflict with Western notions of liberty.

notes: Both agents oppose the statement overall. Agent A starts with a rights-forward disagreement, then moderates by emphasizing Indonesian collective harmony and crisis restrictions while still warning that silencing individual voices can fragment society. Agent B remains consistently rights-first and becomes firmer by the final turn. There is no clear convergence toward either agent; the dialogue mostly sharpens a shared anti-absolute-society-priority stance.
